message:
🐛 Fix 26 runtime errors on dashboard: detect Safari chunk load failures (#2621)

The GA4 error monitor detected 26 runtime errors on `/` — all caused by
Safari/WebKit's "Importing a module script failed" error when loading
stale chunks after a deploy. This error message was not recognized by
isChunkLoadError(), so:

1. ChunkErrorBoundary did not catch it (no auto-reload)
2. The global window 'error' handler logged it as 'runtime' instead of
   'chunk_load', missing the auto-reload recovery path entirely

Fixes:
- Add Safari's "Importing a module script failed" to isChunkLoadError()
- Extract isChunkLoadMessage() for string-based detection (window error
  events provide a message string, not an Error object)
- Add chunk-load detection + auto-reload to the global error and
  unhandledrejection handlers (same throttle logic as ChunkErrorBoundary)

This ensures Safari users get the same auto-reload recovery that
Chrome/Firefox users already had.

Fixes #2601

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
